title: Litigation Evidence Management System Review: EvidencePro vs CaseLocker 2026 Comparison
description: Compare EvidencePro and CaseLocker litigation evidence management systems. Analyze evidence collection, evidence chain management, evidence display, and ev.
content:
Litigation Evidence Management System Overview

Litigation evidence management systems help lawyers organize, manage, and display case evidence. In 2026, EvidencePro and CaseLocker are two leading professional products. This review compares their evidence management features.

Evidence Collection Features

Collection FeaturesEvidenceProCaseLocker
Evidence EntryMultiple format support, batch importBasic format entry
Evidence TagsMulti-dimensional tags, smart classificationSingle tags
Evidence TimelineVisual timeline displayBasic time sorting
Evidence AssociationAuto association analysisManual association

Evidence Chain Management

EvidencePro provides complete evidence chain tracking, evidence source recording, and evidence integrity verification. CaseLocker has basic evidence chain features. For strict evidence chain management, EvidencePro is recommended.

Evidence Display

EvidencePro supports courtroom evidence display, evidence visualization, and evidence animation demonstration. CaseLocker has basic display features. For courtroom presentation, EvidencePro is recommended for litigation lawyers.

Evidence Sharing

EvidencePro supports team collaboration sharing, client evidence sharing, and expert evidence sharing. CaseLocker has limited sharing features. For team collaboration litigation, EvidencePro's sharing solution is recommended.

Mobile Support

EvidencePro has complete mobile support for field evidence collection. CaseLocker mobile is limited to viewing. For field evidence collection, EvidencePro is recommended.

Price and Selection

EvidencePro annual fee: 120,000 CNY; CaseLocker annual fee: 60,000 CNY. For complex litigation and team collaboration, choose EvidencePro. For simple cases with limited budget, choose CaseLocker.
author: Litigation Lawyer Consultant

mainEntity.name: What features should I look for in legal document management software?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key features include document automation, version control, search capabilities with OCR, security compliance (SOC 2, HIPAA), workflow management, e-signature integration, and mobile access for remote work.

mainEntity.name: How much does legal document management software typically cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pricing ranges from $20-50/user/month for basic plans to $100-300/user/month for enterprise solutions. Implementation costs vary based on customization needs and training requirements.

mainEntity.name: What security standards should legal document software meet?

acceptedAnswer.text: Look for SOC 2 Type II compliance, encryption at rest and in transit, audit trails, role-based access control, and integration with firm-wide security policies.

mainEntity.name: Can legal document management integrate with existing law firm systems?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most platforms offer API integrations with practice management software, CRM systems, billing platforms, and Microsoft Office/Google Workspace.

mainEntity.name: What are the main benefits of document automation for law firms?

acceptedAnswer.text: Benefits include reduced drafting time (40-60%), improved accuracy, consistent formatting, faster client turnaround, and reduced compliance risks.

howto_schema.name: How to Choose Litigation Evidence Management System Review: EvidencePro vs CaseLocker 2026

step.name: Assess Requirements
step.text: Evaluate firm size, document volume, compliance needs, and budget to define requirements.

step.name: Research Platforms
step.text: Compare vendors based on features, security, integration capabilities, and user reviews.

step.name: Request Demos
step.text: Schedule demonstrations with 2-3 top vendors to see real workflows and ask questions.

step.name: Check Compliance
step.text: Verify SOC 2, HIPAA, and other relevant certifications; review audit reports.

step.name: Plan Implementation
step.text: Define timeline, data migration strategy, training plan, and success metrics.
